docs.whatIsIntro1: Velanto is a builder for elimination-style quizzes, called packs. A creator picks a topic and one of five formats, adds items, and publishes. Anyone can play a pack as many times as they like, and any round that draws at random pulls a fresh sample each run.
docs.whatIsIntro2: Play is blind: while you're playing, nothing shows you how anyone else picked. When you finish, each round opens up — the items other players picked most, and how many of them agreed with you.
docs.buildCardBody: Pick a format, build pools of items, lay out rounds.
docs.playCardBody: No influence from other players' choices.
docs.compareCardBody: Stats unlock only once you've finished.
docs.creatingIntro: Every pack pairs a topic with exactly one format. You build it in two layers:
docs.formatSaveOneDesc: A group of items appears and you keep exactly one — one pick per round, then the round ends.
docs.formatSacrificeOneDesc: The mirror of Save One: the same one-pick-per-round shape, except you're choosing what to give up rather than what to keep.
docs.formatRankBlindDesc: Items arrive one at a time and you slot each into a ranked list as it appears. You can see how many places the list has, but never what's coming next.
docs.formatNxnDesc: Two pools go head to head. Each round draws a side from each, and you pick a whole side to advance.
docs.format1v1Desc: One item from each of two pools, one match at a time — no bracket, just a run of head-to-heads.
docs.playingIntro: Open any pack and press Play. You'll go round by round following the logic of its format, with no visibility into how anyone else played. At the end you get a personal artifact: a final ranking, a favorite, or a saved set, depending on the format.
docs.playingOutro: You can replay any pack as many times as you like. Rounds built on random slots resample every run, so those packs won't play out the same way twice; a pack built entirely from pinned items plays identically each time.
docs.statsBody: While you're playing, nothing tells you how others picked, so your choices stay your own. Once you finish, each round opens up: the items players picked most, and the percentage who agreed with your pick. A pack's overall agreement score is also shown on its card and detail page before you play.
docs.scopeDesc_packsRead: View your own packs, including ones awaiting approval.
docs.scopeDesc_packsWrite: Create new packs and update your existing ones.
docs.scopeDesc_moderation: Review the moderation queue and reports, and approve or reject packs. Only works if your account is staff.
docs.scopeDesc_profileRead: Read your own profile and settings.
docs.tokenCreateError: Couldn’t create the token. Please try again.
docs.tokenListEmpty: You haven’t created any tokens yet.
docs.tokenCreatedWarning: This is the only time you’ll see this token. Copy it now and store it somewhere safe — if you lose it, revoke it and create a new one.
docs.tokenRevokeWarning: “{name}” will stop working immediately. Any app using it will lose access. This can’t be undone.
docs.tokenRevokeError: Couldn’t revoke the token. Please try again.
docs.creatingPoolDesc: — named bags of items. A pool is just a bag; it doesn't decide how many items appear or when.
docs.creatingRoundDesc: — what a player actually sees, in order. Each round takes its items from a pool through one or more slots.
docs.creatingModesPara: A slot fills one of two ways. A random slot draws a set number of items from its pool and reshuffles them on every play. A manual slot shows exactly the items you chose, in the order you chose them — pinned, so no random slot anywhere in the pack can draw them. Random slots that share a pool also never repeat an item across rounds.
docs.creatingLimitsPara: Elimination rounds draw between 2 and 8 items. Versus formats use exactly two pools, the same two in every round: NxN shows up to 8 items per side, and 1v1 is locked to one per side.
docs.creatingItemsPara: Items can be text, an image upload, or a YouTube link — each with a title.
docs.creatingPublishPara: Publishing sends your pack to moderation rather than straight to the feed. It goes live once a moderator approves it, and you'll see the reason if it's rejected. Editing a pack that's already approved sends it back through moderation. Trusted and staff accounts skip the queue.
docs.statsAnonNote: You can play signed out, and your play still counts toward the pack's stats. It just isn't tied to your account, so it won't appear in your recently played.
docs.apiIntro: Velanto has a REST API you can drive with a personal access token. The same token connects an AI assistant to your account over MCP, so it can draft and manage packs for you. Tokens belong to you alone — treat one like a password.
docs.apiScopesIntro: Every token carries an explicit list of scopes. There's no catch-all scope: a token can only do what you ticked.
docs.apiScopesNarrowNote: A scope only ever narrows what your account can already do — it never grants anything extra. Giving a token the moderation scope on an account that isn't staff gets it nothing.
docs.apiMcpIntro: The velanto-mcp server bridges any MCP-capable assistant to your account. Create a token above, then add this to your assistant's MCP config:
docs.apiMcpToolsIntro: That gives the assistant these tools:
docs.apiMcpScopeNote: Each tool needs the matching scope on your token, so an assistant can only reach what you allowed. Packs it creates enter moderation like any other.
docs.apiDirectIntro: Send your token as a bearer token on any request:
docs.apiDirectNote: A token can't create or revoke tokens — that always needs a real sign-in, so a leaked token can't extend itself.

rules.metaDescription: The community rules for creating and playing packs on Velanto — read them before you register.
rules.heading: Community Rules
rules.intro: These rules keep Velanto safe and fair for everyone. By creating an account you agree to follow them.
rules.loadError: We couldn't load the rules right now. Please try again in a moment.

items.1: Do not attack, dehumanize, demean, or promote hatred against any person or group based on race, ethnicity, national origin, religion, gender, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, serious medical condition, age, or any comparable protected characteristic. This applies to text, images, usernames, pack content, comments, and any other contribution.
items.2: Do not use slurs, hateful symbols, or coded language intended to degrade a protected group, and do not post imagery that celebrates or endorses hateful ideologies.
items.3: Do not advocate for, or express support for, discrimination, segregation, exclusion, or the denial of rights to people because of a protected characteristic.
items.4: Do not create packs, questions, or answers whose premise ranks, mocks, or asserts the inferiority of a protected group. Legitimate factual or educational content about history, culture, or religion is permitted only when presented respectfully and without endorsing hatred.

items.1: Do not target another person with insults, mockery, intimidation, or sustained unwanted contact, whether through packs, comments, usernames, or any other feature.
items.2: Do not organize, encourage, or participate in coordinated harassment, brigading, or pile-ons against any individual or group.
items.3: Do not post content designed to shame, humiliate, or provoke a specific person, including content referencing their appearance, identity, or personal circumstances.
items.4: Respect requests to stop contact. Continuing to engage a user who has asked to be left alone, or evading a block to reach them, is prohibited.

items.1: Do not threaten violence against, or wish serious harm upon, any person or group, and do not incite others to commit violence.
items.2: Do not glorify, promote, or provide instructions for terrorism, mass violence, or violent extremism, and do not praise the perpetrators of such acts.
items.3: Do not encourage, promote, or provide instructions for suicide, self-harm, or eating disorders. If you or someone you know is at risk, seek help from local emergency services or a crisis line.
items.4: Do not post gratuitous gore, graphic depictions of death or serious injury, or imagery intended to shock or celebrate suffering.
items.5: Do not use the platform to plan, coordinate, or recruit for acts of real-world violence.

items.1: Do not post pornography, sexually explicit imagery, or depictions of sexual acts anywhere on the platform.
items.2: Do not post nudity or sexualized content in packs, images, usernames, avatars, or comments. This is a general-audience platform.
items.3: Do not use the platform to solicit sexual contact, distribute sexual content, or advertise sexual services.
items.4: Do not share intimate or sexual images of any person without their explicit consent, including so-called "revenge" content.

items.1: We have ZERO tolerance for the sexualization of minors. Any content that sexualizes, exploits, or endangers a person under 18 — real, fictional, illustrated, or AI-generated — results in immediate permanent account termination and, where applicable, reporting to the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC) and relevant law enforcement.
items.2: Do not upload, request, share, or link to child sexual abuse material (CSAM) or any sexualized depiction of a minor under any circumstances.
items.3: Do not attempt to contact, groom, sexualize, or otherwise endanger a minor, and do not use the platform to facilitate such conduct.
items.4: Report any suspected child endangerment immediately. We cooperate fully with authorities and preserve relevant evidence as required by law.

items.1: Do not use the platform to facilitate, promote, or coordinate activity that is illegal in your jurisdiction or the jurisdiction where the platform operates.
items.2: Do not advertise, sell, or facilitate the trade of illegal goods or services, including illicit drugs, weapons, stolen data, or counterfeit items.
items.3: Do not post instructions for producing weapons, explosives, drugs, or other materials whose primary purpose is to cause serious harm or commit a crime.
items.4: Do not use the platform to launder money, run financial scams, or otherwise engage in fraud or theft.

items.1: Do not publish another person's private or identifying information — such as home address, phone number, government ID, financial details, or private communications — without their explicit consent.
items.2: Do not threaten to expose private information, and do not encourage or coordinate the exposure of anyone’s private information.
items.3: Do not share information obtained through hacking, unauthorized access, or a breach of confidence.
items.4: Only share information about yourself that you are comfortable being public. Take care not to reveal others’ personal details in packs, comments, or media.

items.1: Do not impersonate another person, brand, organization, or Velanto staff, including through usernames, avatars, or profile details designed to mislead.
items.2: Do not run scams, phishing schemes, or deceptive practices intended to trick users into giving up money, credentials, or personal information.
items.3: Do not misrepresent your affiliation with, or endorsement by, any person or organization.
items.4: Parody or fan content is permitted only when it is clearly labeled and could not reasonably be mistaken for the real person or entity.

items.1: Do not post repetitive, unsolicited, or bulk content, and do not flood the platform with low-value packs, comments, or messages.
items.2: Do not manipulate votes, ratings, popularity, or play counts through bots, alternate accounts, coordinated groups, or any artificial means.
items.3: Do not use automated scripts, bots, or exploits to interact with the platform in ways that undermine fair use or distort metrics.
items.4: Do not cheat, exploit scoring, or otherwise gain an unfair advantage in plays or competitions.
items.5: Do not post deceptive links, redirect users to unexpected destinations, or advertise unrelated products and services.

items.1: Do not post false or misleading information that could cause real-world harm, such as dangerous health claims, fabricated safety guidance, or content that undermines public safety.
items.2: Do not use the platform to spread coordinated disinformation, fabricated evidence, or manipulated media intended to deceive.
items.3: Do not present false factual claims as verified answers in packs. Trivia should be accurate and, where relevant, sourced; opinion and satire should be clearly distinguishable from fact.

items.1: Do not upload, reproduce, or distribute text, images, audio, or other material that you do not own or have the right to use.
items.2: Respect copyright, trademarks, and licensing terms. Give attribution where required and honor the conditions of any license you rely on.
items.3: Do not post content that infringes another party’s intellectual property. We respond to valid takedown requests and may remove infringing content and penalize repeat infringers.

items.1: Do not evade bans, suspensions, or other enforcement actions by creating new accounts or using alternate identities.
items.2: Do not probe for, exploit, or abuse bugs, vulnerabilities, or unintended behavior. Report security issues responsibly instead of exploiting them.
items.3: Do not attempt to circumvent moderation, content filters, rate limits, or access controls.
items.4: Do not interfere with the normal operation of the platform, including through denial-of-service attempts, scraping in violation of these rules, or unauthorized access to systems or data.

terms.metaDescription: The terms that govern your use of Velanto.
terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: This is the agreement between you and the person who runs Velanto. It is written to be read, not to be skipped — it is short, and it says what you can expect from us and what we expect from you.

sections.title: Who we are
sections.body: Velanto is a free platform for making and playing elimination quiz packs. It is run by one independent developer based in Ukraine, in their own time. It is not a company. When these Terms say “we”, that is who they mean. You can reach us at support@playvelanto.com — one person reads that inbox.

sections.title: Who may use Velanto
sections.body: You must be 16 or older to have an account. When you register, you confirm that you are. We take your word for it: we do not ask for proof and we do not collect your date of birth. If we learn that an account belongs to someone under 16, we close it. We do not go looking.

sections.title: Your account
sections.body: You register with an email address, a username and a password. We send a six-digit code to that address, and you must enter it before the account is created — there is no such thing as an unverified Velanto account. Keep your password to yourself; you are responsible for what happens under your account. If you think someone else has got into it, change your password and tell us.

sections.title: Your content stays yours
sections.body: You own every pack, comment and vote you make. So that the site can show your content to other people, you give us a non-exclusive, worldwide licence to host, store, display and distribute it — for the purpose of running Velanto, and for nothing else. We do not sell it, license it onward, or use it to advertise. You are responsible for what you post, and you confirm you have the right to post it.

sections.title: What you may not post
sections.body: The Community Rules are part of this agreement and they have the detail. In short: nothing illegal, nothing hateful, nothing that harasses another person, nothing that infringes someone else's rights, and nothing that abuses or disrupts the service or other people's accounts.

sections.title: How moderation actually works
sections.body: Two things stand between a submission and the site, and it is worth knowing exactly what each does:
bullets: A word filter runs the moment you submit. It blocks the submission outright — blocked content never reaches our database at all. It does not flag you, report you, or ban you. It only says no.

A person reviews packs before they appear. Every pack from an ordinary account is reviewed before publication. Accounts we have marked as trusted, and staff accounts, skip that review and publish immediately.
Every ban is a decision made by a person. Nothing on Velanto bans you automatically.

sections.title: When we remove things or close accounts
sections.body: We remove content, restrict features, or suspend or close an account when there is a serious reason to: a breach of these Terms or the Community Rules, or a real risk to other users or to the service. We try to match what we do to how serious the problem is, and we act immediately only where waiting would cause harm. We do not close accounts arbitrarily, and if we end your access for a reason that is not a breach — if we stop running Velanto, for instance — we give you reasonable notice first. If you think we got it wrong, write to support@playvelanto.com and a person will look again.

sections.title: API tokens and AI agents
sections.body: You can create API tokens on the documentation page and give them to software you choose, including AI agents. A token acts as you, within the scopes you pick when you create it — and some of those scopes can create, change and delete your packs.
bullets: Anything done with your token is your responsibility, the same as if you had done it yourself.
Whatever software you give the token to can see everything that token is allowed to read. Only give one to software you trust.
A token never expires unless you set an expiry. Delete the ones you are not using.

sections.title: Other people's content
sections.body: Packs are written by users, not by us. A pack can embed a YouTube video — that is the only way another company can be reached through a pack, and the only thing a pack can point at that we do not host ourselves. We do not control what is on YouTube and are not responsible for it, and watching it there is governed by Google’s terms, not ours. If something on Velanto infringes your rights, tell us and we will look at it.

sections.title: What Velanto is, and what it is not
sections.body: Velanto is a free project run by one person in their spare time. There is no company behind it, no support desk, no uptime guarantee and no service-level agreement. It can go down, lose data, change, or stop existing. Please do not rely on it for anything that matters — it is a place to make quizzes. We say this plainly because it is the honest description of the service, and because what you can fairly expect from Velanto is measured against what Velanto actually is.

sections.title: Our responsibility to you
sections.body: Nothing in these Terms limits or excludes our liability for intent, gross negligence, death or personal injury, or any other liability that cannot be limited or excluded under the law that applies to you. That sentence outranks everything else in this section. Beyond it: we are not liable for damage caused by ordinary carelessness, except where your national law does not allow that to be excluded. We also do not warrant that user-written packs are accurate — other users wrote them, not us. If you are a consumer, you keep every mandatory right your own law gives you, and nothing here takes any of them away.

sections.title: Ending this agreement
sections.body: You can delete your account whenever you like, from your settings. You do not need a reason and you do not need to ask us. It costs nothing. We can end it as described above, and our Privacy Policy explains exactly what happens to your data when an account goes.

sections.title: Changes to these Terms
sections.body: Velanto changes, so these Terms will change too. When we make a change that actually affects you, we will tell you before it takes effect and give you reasonable notice. If you do not want to accept the new Terms, delete your account — that is free, immediate and always available to you. Corrections that do not affect your rights we simply make, and update the date at the top.

sections.title: Which law applies
sections.body: These Terms are governed by the law of Ukraine, where Velanto is run. If you are a consumer, that choice does not strip you of anything: you keep the mandatory protections of the country you live in, and you can bring a claim in your own country's courts. Where your national law says something different from these Terms, your national law wins.

sections.title: Language
sections.body: These Terms were written in English, and the English version is the one that governs. The other translations are machine-made and offered to help you read them. If a translation and the English disagree, the English text is the agreement.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: Questions about these Terms? Write to support@playvelanto.com.

privacy.metaDescription: What Velanto collects, why, how long it keeps it, and what you can do about it.
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This explains what Velanto collects, why, who else sees it, and how long it stays. It is more detailed than most policies because the honest answers are more interesting than the usual ones.

sections.title: The short version
sections.body: The detail is below, but if you read only this much:
bullets: We collect what you give us and what the site needs to work. Nothing else.
No ads, no analytics, no tracking, no profiling. Nothing is sold to anyone.
We do not store your IP address.
If you are not logged in, your play is still counted, but it is saved with nothing that links it to you.
You can export your data, or delete your account, yourself — buttons in settings, no request needed.
Deleting your account does not erase everything you ever wrote. What survives, and in what form, is listed below.

sections.title: Who is responsible for your data
sections.body: Velanto is run by one independent developer based in Ukraine. That person decides what is collected and why, which makes them the data controller. There is no company, no data protection officer, and no team — just an inbox: support@playvelanto.com. The rights described in this policy are given to everyone who uses Velanto, wherever you live. We do not check your country first and we do not give some users fewer rights than others.

sections.title: What we collect
sections.body: Almost all of it is something you typed:
bullets: Account — your email address, username, and password. The password is hashed with argon2, which means we cannot read it and it is never included in an export.
Profile — your bio and avatar, if you add them.
What you make — packs, comments, votes, reports and feedback.
Plays — which packs were played and what was picked. Tied to your account only when you are logged in; a signed-out play is saved with no player attached to it.
Follows, notifications, and any API tokens you create — for a token we keep its name, the scopes you gave it, when it expires and when it was last used, never the token itself.
The version of the Community Rules you accepted when you registered, and any moderation flags on your account — whether we have marked you trusted, and whether you are banned and why.
Technical — a login session, and a crash report if something breaks.

sections.title: Why we use it, and our legal basis
sections.body: Every use has a reason and a legal basis. We are naming them because a policy that does not is hiding something:
bullets: Running your account, showing your content, and letting you play — because that is our agreement with you (performance of a contract, GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)). Without this data there is no account.
Sending your verification code and any password-reset code — the same reason. You asked for the account; it cannot work otherwise.

Moderating content, handling reports, keeping the service secure, and logging what staff do — because we, and everyone else using Velanto, have a legitimate interest in a service that is not abused (Art. 6(1)(f)).
Receiving crash reports — a legitimate interest in a service that works (Art. 6(1)(f)).
Keeping something because a law requires it (Art. 6(1)(c)).

We do not ask your consent for any of this, and you should be suspicious of us if we did: consent is not what we rely on. Your account is a contract, and everything else rests on legitimate interests — which you can object to at any time, as described further down.

sections.title: If you are not logged in, we do not know who you are
sections.body: You can browse and play packs without an account. Your play does count toward the pack’s public stats, but it is saved with no player attached: no account, no name, nothing that links it back to you. It never appears in anyone’s play history, because there is nothing to attach it to. If you are signed in, your play is saved with your account instead, and you can hide your play history from other people in your settings.

sections.title: What we do not do
sections.body: This list is as much a part of the policy as the rest:
bullets: No advertising, and no ad networks.
No analytics. Not Google Analytics, not a privacy-friendly alternative, not anything. We do not measure you.
No profiling and no personalized feed. What is popular is popular for everyone, calculated the same way for every viewer.
No special-category data. We never ask your date of birth, gender, location, politics, religion, or health, and there is nowhere to put them.

No IP address stored. Our server does not log requests and there is no IP column in the database. Rate limiting holds an address in memory for sixty seconds and then forgets it. Cloudflare, which passes traffic to the site, does see visitors' addresses — see the list of processors below.
Fonts are served from our own server, so loading a Velanto page tells Google nothing.

sections.title: Cookies and what is stored in your browser
sections.body: There is no cookie banner. Everything stored is either needed to make the site work or is a setting you chose, and none of it follows you to other websites — so there is nothing here we would ask you to opt into. One honest note before the list: the login cookie lasts 30 days rather than clearing when you close the browser, and reasonable people think a cookie that persistent should be your choice rather than our default. We think so too, and a "keep me signed in" option is on our list. Here is the complete list:
bullets: Your login cookie — this is what keeps you signed in. It lasts 30 days and it survives closing your browser, so on a shared computer, log out rather than just closing the tab. JavaScript cannot read it.
NEXT_LOCALE — your interface language. One year.
velanto:accent, velanto:streamer-mode, velanto:pack-filters — display settings you chose. They never leave your browser.
velanto:last-play — your picks from the pack you just played, so the result screen can show them. Cleared when you close the tab.

velanto:otp-sent — a sixty-second timer that stops you requesting another code too quickly. It holds a scrambled fingerprint of the address rather than the address itself, and expired entries are dropped the next time the page reads or writes the timer.
Your access token is kept in memory only. It is never written to disk.

sections.title: YouTube embeds — where a third party sees you
sections.body: Packs can embed YouTube videos, and we keep YouTube at arm's length as far as we can — no player is built when the page loads, only a still image. Three things are still worth knowing. That still image is fetched from YouTube's servers, so YouTube sees a request whenever a pack containing a video is displayed. The player then loads as soon as your pointer passes over the video — you do not have to click, though tapping does the same on a phone — and from that moment YouTube receives your request directly and can set its own cookies. And if you paste a YouTube link while making a pack, your browser asks YouTube for the video's title. What YouTube does with any of that is governed by Google's privacy policy, not ours. Apart from choosing to sign in with Google or Discord, this is the only part of Velanto where another company can observe you.

sections.title: Images you upload
sections.body: Uploaded images pass through our server to Amazon S3 in Frankfurt, and are delivered through CloudFront. Three things are worth knowing:
bullets: We strip metadata. Your camera model and any GPS coordinates in the original file are removed when we re-encode the image — they never reach storage.

The URL is public. Anyone with the link can open the image without logging in, including before a moderator has seen it. The link is unguessable, but it is not secret. Treat anything you upload as public.
We do not scan images automatically for malware or adult content. A person reviews them as part of pack moderation.

sections.title: Who else touches your data
sections.body: A few companies, each doing one job. This is the complete list — nobody else receives anything. The last two only come into it if you choose to sign in with them:
bullets: Amazon Web Services (S3 and CloudFront) — stores and delivers uploaded images. Frankfurt, in the EU.
Amazon SES — sends the only two emails we send. Each contains a six-digit code and nothing else: no username, no link, no IP address.

Sentry — receives a report when something crashes. Germany, in the EU. A report carries your user id and username, plus a short trail of what you had just done: the pages you visited, what you clicked, and which addresses the app called (some of which name a pack). It does not carry your email address, your cookies, or what you typed, and there is no video-style session recording. One limit on that promise: like any server your browser talks to, Sentry's sees the connection itself, so it can observe the address you connect from even though we never put it in the report.

Cloudflare — DNS, and the traffic to the site passes through it. Like any provider sitting in front of a website, it sees the address every visitor connects from. We do not receive it and we do not store it, but we will not pretend it is invisible to everyone.

Google — only if you choose “Continue with Google”. During that sign-in Google sees that you are logging into Velanto and confirms your email address to us, so we can create or find your account; we receive nothing else about you. What Google does with the sign-in is governed by Google's privacy policy.

Discord — only if you choose “Continue with Discord”. During that sign-in Discord sees that you are logging into Velanto and confirms who you are, so we can create or find your account. We do not ask Discord for your email or anything else about you — only enough to identify the account. Governed by Discord's privacy policy.

sections.title: Where your data is
sections.body: Storage and error reporting are in the EU: S3 in Frankfurt, Sentry in Germany. CloudFront and Cloudflare deliver from locations around the world, which is how any website loads quickly, so a copy of a page or an image can sit briefly on a server outside the EU. Those two run on their providers' standard data-protection terms, which is the safeguard we rely on for it. The person who runs Velanto is in Ukraine and can access the data — that is unavoidable when one person runs a service, and you should know it rather than guess it. If you sign in with Google or Discord, that sign-in happens with those companies' servers, which are outside the EU.

sections.title: How long we keep things
sections.body: This is the section most policies get wrong, so read it. Most of your data does not disappear when you leave — it is detached from your name instead, so that the site still makes sense to everyone else. Precisely:
bullets: Your account, profile and avatar — until you delete the account. Then 30 days. Then gone.
Your packs — deleted with your account. Be aware this also deletes other people's comments, votes and plays on them.
Your comments — kept, with your name removed. The text stays so that conversations still make sense to the people who were in them.
Your feedback posts — the same: kept, detached from you.
Your votes — kept as numbers with no name attached, so that pack scores stay correct.
Your play records — kept, detached from you.

The action log — kept indefinitely, and it survives your account being deleted. It records moderation decisions: bans, pack approvals, and a staff member taking down something that is not theirs. Editing or deleting your own pack or your own comment is not recorded. Keeping the moderation entries is the entire point of it: being the subject of a moderation action does not let you erase the record of it. If you were staff, deleting your account takes your name off the entries you made, but not the entries themselves.
Verification and password-reset codes — deleted the moment they are used, an hour after they expire if you never use them, and with your account if one is still in flight when you leave.
Login sessions — they stop working when they expire, and we delete the expired ones within the hour.
In one line: deleting your account removes you from Velanto and unlinks your name from what you left behind. It does not erase everything you ever wrote.

sections.title: Deleting your account
sections.body: It is a button in your settings, and you need your current password to use it. Then:
bullets: Your account stops working straight away.
For 30 days you can undo it — just log in again and it comes back. This is deliberate: it protects you from a bad moment, or from someone else getting into your account and deleting it.
After 30 days a nightly job destroys it for good, following the list above.

One thing worth knowing: for up to fifteen minutes after you delete your account, a sign-in token already issued to your browser still works. That is how the technology works rather than an oversight — the token is a signed note with an expiry, not a key we can reach out and break. Everything else stops immediately.

sections.title: Moderation, reports, and the audit log
sections.body: We record moderation decisions — a staff member approving a pack, banning someone, resolving a report, or taking down something that is not theirs — noting who did it and what they did, and we keep it (see how long, above). Editing or deleting your own pack or your own comment is not recorded at all. One part of this deserves to be said out loud rather than buried:
bullets: Reports are not anonymous to moderators. If you report a pack or a person, the moderator handling it sees your username. The person you reported does not.

sections.title: Your rights
sections.body: You have all of these, wherever you live, and you do not have to email us to use the first four:
bullets: See your data — settings has an export button. It produces a file immediately: your account and profile, your packs in full (every pool, item and round, so you can rebuild them elsewhere), your comments, votes, plays, feedback, follows, the reports you filed, your notifications and their settings, your API tokens, your sign-in sessions, and any moderation flags on your account. No request, no waiting, no identity check.
Correct it — edit your profile and your content directly.
Delete it — settings has a delete-account button, described above.
Take it elsewhere — that is the same export, as a JSON file.

Ask us to pause — if you think our data about you is wrong, or you have objected and we are still thinking about it, you can ask us to stop using it in the meantime while leaving it in place. Write to support@playvelanto.com.
Complain — see below.

Two things the export deliberately leaves out, and always will: your password and the secret half of any API token. We cannot include them because we do not have them — both are stored only as hashes we cannot reverse. Everything else we hold about you is in the file.
If you want something the buttons do not do, write to support@playvelanto.com. We answer within 30 days.

sections.title: Your right to object
sections.body: You have the right to object, at any time, to any use of your data that rests on our legitimate interests — that means the moderation, the security measures, the audit log and the crash reporting described above. Write to support@playvelanto.com. You do not need to give a reason and it costs nothing. If you object, we stop, unless we can demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds that override your interests, rights and freedoms. This right is separate from everything else in this policy, which is why it has its own section.

sections.title: Complaining about us
sections.body: Tell us first — support@playvelanto.com — and we will genuinely try to put it right. You do not have to, though. You can complain directly to a data protection authority: in the EU or EEA, that is the authority where you live, where you work, or where you think the problem happened, and the list is at edpb.europa.eu. In Ukraine, it is the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights.

sections.title: If you are under 16
sections.body: You need to be 16 to have a Velanto account, and you confirm that you are when you register. We take your word for it: we do not ask for proof, we do not collect your date of birth, and we do not try to guess your age from anything. If we find out an account belongs to someone under 16, we close it and delete it. If you are a parent and you think your child has an account here, write to support@playvelanto.com and we will remove it.

sections.title: Security
sections.body: Passwords are hashed with argon2 — we cannot read yours, and it is never exported. Login sessions use single-use tokens: each time one is used it is revoked and replaced, so a stolen token has a short life. The site sets standard security headers, and one that switches off the browser's advertising-topics API. No system is perfectly secure and we will not pretend otherwise. Velanto is run by one person, and you should weigh that when deciding what to put here.

sections.title: Changes to this policy
sections.body: We will update this as Velanto changes. When a change matters to you, we will tell you before it takes effect and update the date at the top. If you do not like a change, you can delete your account at any time, for free.

sections.title: Language
sections.body: This policy was written in English, and the English version is the one that governs. The other translations are machine-made and offered to help you read it. If a translation and the English disagree, the English text is the one that counts.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: Anything in this policy, including every right listed above: support@playvelanto.com. One person reads that inbox, and they wrote this page.

save_one.title: A group's pool appears

save_one.title: You save one

save_one.title: Next group, same deal
save_one.desc: One save per group, across every round — no going back to change a pick.

sacrifice_one.title: A group's pool appears
sacrifice_one.desc: Each round shows a group's items to choose between.

sacrifice_one.title: You sacrifice one

sacrifice_one.title: Next group, same deal

nxn.title: Picks tally up
nxn.desc: Your choices add up across all rounds to a final result.

rank_blind.title: No do-overs
rank_blind.desc: Your ranking locks in once every item is placed.

1v1.title: Pick the winner

1v1.title: Winners tally up
